assertion_results.text: EOSE state is tracked per-relay per-subscription (not globally) using a map or similar structure
assertion_results.evidence:
Found 10 match(es) for keyword "eose" (heuristic match):
  subscription-manager.ts:5: * - Per-relay EOSE tracking with overall loading → live transition
  subscription-manager.ts:6: * - EOSE timeout to prevent infinite loading states
  subscription-manager.ts:20: eoseReceived: boolean;

assertion_results.text: The overall subscription transitions to 'live' only after all relays have sent EOSE or a timeout has elapsed
assertion_results.evidence: No matches for "sent eose or a timeout has elapsed" found across 1 output file(s)

assertion_results.text: An EOSE timeout mechanism exists (e.g., setTimeout) that forces transition to live if relays are slow

assertion_results.text: CLOSED messages are parsed and handled differently based on reason prefix (at least auth-required and restricted)
For auth-required CLOSED, the code attempts re-authentication before re-subscribing
Subscription replacement is supported by sending a new REQ with the same subscription ID and resetting EOSE tracking
assertion_results.evidence:
Found 10 match(es) for keyword "subscription" (heuristic match):
  subscription-manager.ts:2: * Subscription Manager for Nostr clients
  subscription-manager.ts:4: * Handles the complete subscription lifecycle:
  subscription-manager.ts:8: * - Subscription replacement with same sub-id

assertion_results.text: After relay reconnection, subscriptions are re-sent with a 'since' filter parameter based on the last EOSE timestamp
Subscriptions can be closed by sending CLOSE messages to all connected relays
